name: Sahara Desert — Merzouga & Erg Chebbi

seo.title: Sahara Desert in Merzouga — Erg Chebbi, camp, camels | Authentic Morocco Tours
seo.description: Discover Morocco's Sahara desert in Merzouga: Erg Chebbi dunes, a night in a desert camp under the stars, camel rides, gnawa music. Private tours from Marrakech or Fes.

hero.tagline: Golden dunes, starry nights and endless silence at the gates of the Sahara

intro: Erg Chebbi, in Merzouga, is the most spectacular accessible dune field in Morocco: some peaks rise over 150 metres. This is where the quintessential Saharan experience happens — a camel ride at sunset, a night in a desert camp under a sky saturated with stars, and encounters with nomadic families and the gnawa music of Khamlia village. 9 hours' drive from Marrakech or 6 hours from Fes, Merzouga is best reached over 2 to 3 days, with stops in the Atlas and the Ziz Valley. Along the way, the road crosses some of the Kingdom's most varied landscapes: the Tichka Pass or Middle Atlas cedar forests, the Todra Gorges, the Dades Valley and its kasbahs, the Skoura palm groves. It is this constant contrast — mountain, oasis, stone and sand — that makes the desert tour Morocco's most popular itinerary, for couples and families alike.

highlights.name: Erg Chebbi
highlights.description: Morocco's tallest dune field, between 40 and 150 m, famous for its changing colours at sunrise and sunset.

highlights.name: Camp under the stars
highlights.description: A night in a Berber tent in the open desert, a traditional dinner and an exceptionally clear sky, far from any light pollution.

highlights.name: Khamlia and gnawa music
highlights.description: A small village south of Merzouga, known for its gnawa musicians descended from sub-Saharan slaves and their mesmerising percussion.

highlights.name: Ziz Valley
highlights.description: On the road to Merzouga, a palm grove and spectacular gorges to cross, with panoramic views over the oasis.

practicalInfo.bestPeriod: March to May and September to November (extreme heat in summer, cool nights in winter)
practicalInfo.daysRecommended: 2 to 3 days minimum from Marrakech or Fes, to enjoy at least one night in a desert camp
tips: Bring warm clothes for the night, even in strong daytime heat
The camel ride is usually done at sunset and/or sunrise
Prefer a private camp over a shared campsite for more peace and quiet
